title: Mellow Mexican Chicken Mole Poblano
description: A lazy Sunday Mexican dish featuring peppercorns and a rich mole poblano sauce, served in a bento box with nigiri-shaped chicken and raw-friendly ingredients.
instructions: Toast the ancho and mulato chili peppers in a dry skillet over medium heat for 2 minutes.
Grind the toasted chili peppers, peppercorns, almonds, and coriander into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Soak the raisins in water for 10 minutes, then drain and add to the spice grinder.
Grind the mixture into a smooth paste.
Melt the dark chocolate in a double boiler or in the microwave in 30-second increments, stirring between each interval.
Combine the chocolate and spice paste in a blender or food processor and blend until smooth.
Add the chicken pieces to the blender and blend until just combined.
Scoop the chicken mixture into small balls and shape into nigiri shapes.
Place the nigiri-shaped chicken into a bento box and spoon the mole poblano sauce over the top.
Serve immediately, garnished with toasted almonds and raisins.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
